info.name: Survey Platform API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Survey Platform.

**Setup:**
1. Import this collection
2. Run **Login as Admin** first — the `access_token` is automatically saved via the Tests script
3. All authenticated requests use `{{access_token}}` automatically via the collection-level Bearer auth

**Seeded users:** admin@survey.com / analyst@survey.com / viewer@survey.com — all password: `Admin1234!`

**Seeded survey ID:** `10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`

**Note on trailing slashes:** A collection-level pre-request script automatically appends a trailing slash to every request URL. Django raises a 500 on PATCH / POST / PUT requests to slash-less URLs (it cannot safely redirect requests that carry a body).

exec: // Django raises a 500 (RuntimeError) when PATCH / POST / PUT requests are sent
// to a URL without a trailing slash — it cannot safely redirect a request that
// carries a body. This script ensures every request ends with '/' regardless of
// how the URL was typed, so the collection works out of the box.

request.description: Register a new staff user. `role` options: `admin`, `analyst`, `data_viewer` (default if omitted).
Logs in as admin. `access_token` and `refresh_token` are saved automatically to collection variables and used by all subsequent requests.
Logs in as analyst. Can access analytics and trigger exports but cannot create or edit surveys.
Logs in as data_viewer. Can read analytics and export reports but cannot trigger exports or access the survey builder.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. The new access token is saved automatically.
Returns a paginated list of all surveys. Toggle the query params to filter by status, creator, or search by title/description. Admin only.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Customer Satisfaction Survey",
  "description": "Annual feedback survey.",
  "sections": [
    {
      "title": "Personal Info",
      "order": 1,
      "conditions": null,
      "fields": [
        {
          "label": "Full Name",
          "field_type": "text",
          "order": 1,
          "is_required": true,
          "maps_to": "respondent_full_name",
          "validation_rules": {"min_length": 2, "max_length": 100},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Email Address",
          "field_type": "email",
          "order": 2,
          "is_required": true,
          "maps_to": "respondent_email",
          "validation_rules": {"max_length": 254},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Phone Number",
          "field_type": "text",
          "order": 3,
          "is_required": false,
          "maps_to": "respondent_phone",
          "validation_rules": {"regex": "^\\+?[0-9\\s\\-]{7,15}$"},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Do you own a car?",
          "field_type": "radio",
          "order": 4,
          "is_required": true,
          "options": ["Yes", "No"],
          "conditions": null
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Vehicle Details",
      "order": 2,
      "conditions": null,
      "fields": [
        {
          "label": "Car Brand",
          "field_type": "dropdown",
          "order": 1,
          "is_required": true,
          "options": ["Toyota", "Honda", "Ford", "BMW", "Tesla", "Other"],
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Favourite Features",
          "field_type": "checkbox",
          "order": 2,
          "is_required": false,
          "options": ["Safety", "Fuel Economy", "Performance", "Design", "Tech"],
          "validation_rules": {"min_selections": 1, "max_selections": 3},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Year of Manufacture",
          "field_type": "number",
          "order": 3,
          "is_required": true,
          "validation_rules": {"min": 1990, "max": 2025},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Purchase Date",
          "field_type": "date",
          "order": 4,
          "is_required": false,
          "validation_rules": {"min_date": "1990-01-01", "max_date": "2025-12-31"},
          "conditions": null
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "General Feedback",
      "order": 3,
      "conditions": null,
      "fields": [
        {
          "label": "Additional Comments",
          "field_type": "textarea",
          "order": 1,
          "is_required": false,
          "validation_rules": {"max_length": 1000},
          "conditions": null
        },
        {
          "label": "Overall Satisfaction",
          "field_type": "rating",
          "order": 2,
          "is_required": true,
          "validation_rules": {"min": 1, "max": 5},
          "conditions": null
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Creates a survey with nested sections and fields in one atomic request. Survey starts as `draft`.

⚠️ **All `conditions` must be `null` on create** — field UUIDs are generated server-side and are not known yet.

**Step 2:** copy the field UUIDs from the response, then call `PUT` to add conditional logic.

variable.description: Survey UUID returned by the POST

request.description:
Full replace — **all sections and fields not included in this payload are permanently deleted.**

This is step 2: replace the `<uuid-of-*>` placeholders with real field UUIDs from the `POST` response. Use `GET .../fields/` to retrieve the flat list of UUIDs without having to parse the full nested structure.

**Available operators:** `eq` `neq` `gt` `lt` `gte` `lte` `contains` `in`
Returns the full survey structure with all sections and fields including conditions, validation rules, and options. Works for any status (draft / published / archived).

item.name: Get Survey Fields (flat list for conditions builder)

request.description:
Returns every field in the survey as a **flat, ordered list** (by section order then field order). Intended for the conditions builder UI — gives you `id`, `label`, `field_type`, and `options` without having to parse the full nested survey structure.

Use the returned `id` values as `field_id` when building conditions in the `PUT` payload.

Changes the survey status to `published`, making it accessible to respondents.

**Requirements:** at least one section containing at least one field. Idempotent — publishing an already-published survey returns 200.
Permanently deletes the survey and all related data — sections, fields, responses, and export reports.
Returns the full survey structure for respondents. No auth required. Redis-cached for 1 hour. Draft and archived surveys return 404.

exec: console.log('Session token saved to collection variable: session_token');

request.description:
Creates a Respondent + SurveyResponse and returns a JWT `session_token`. Optionally saves first answers.

The `session_token` is **automatically saved** to the `session_token` collection variable by the Tests script — the Resume, Autosave, and Submit requests use it automatically.

Send `{}` or `{"answers": []}` to start with no initial answers.

variable.description: JWT token — auto-populated from Start Session

request.description: Returns all previously saved answers and the session status (`in_progress` or `completed`). Sensitive fields are decrypted transparently.

variable.description: JWT token — auto-populated from Start Session

request.description:
Upserts answers — sending the same `field_id` again updates the existing value rather than creating a duplicate. Updates `last_saved_at`.

Seeded survey field IDs:
- Full Name: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`
- Email Address: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002`
- Do you own a car?: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003`
- Car Brand: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004`
- Year of Manufacture: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005`
- Additional Comments: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000006`
- Overall Satisfaction: `30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000007`

variable.description: JWT token — auto-populated from Start Session

request.description:
`car=Yes` activates the Vehicle Details section — Car Brand and Year of Manufacture are required and must be answered. Respondent profile is auto-populated from `maps_to` fields (Full Name → `respondent.full_name`, Email → `respondent.email`).

Merges answers from this payload with any previously autosaved answers.

variable.description: JWT token — auto-populated from Start Session

request.description: `car=No` means the Vehicle Details section condition fails — Car Brand and Year of Manufacture are NOT enforced even though they are marked required. Submit succeeds without them, demonstrating conditional logic enforcement.

Returns total response count, completed count, completion rate (%), and per-field value distributions for choice fields (radio, dropdown, checkbox).

Redis-cached for 5 minutes. Accessible to admin, analyst, and data_viewer.

exec: console.log('Poll List Export Reports until status = ready');

request.description:
Queues a Celery background task to export all completed survey responses to CSV. Returns immediately with an `export_report_id`.

The `export_report_id` is saved automatically to the collection variable. Poll **List Export Reports** until `status` changes from `pending` to `ready`, then use `file_url` to download the CSV.

Admin and analyst only.

Lists all export reports for this survey, ordered newest first. Toggle the query params to filter by status or requester.

When `status=ready`, `file_url` contains the absolute URL to download the CSV. Accessible to admin, analyst, and data_viewer.
